survivors_flagged.one_line: Dividend-suspended recap underway: dated tender offers for 2026/27 notes, $2B secured-notes refi, plus Tepper/Appaloosa activist campaign on dilution -- forward deleveraging + activist hardening.

Elevate28 holdco-to-single-company overhaul + Burson PR-arm sale exploration (Goldman advisors), GBP500m cost-out by 2028; real structural restructuring but no hard dated arb and a melting top line — WATCH not ACTIVE.
Definitive all-cash acquisition by Ares at $19.00/sh (~$1.7bn), expected Q3-2026 close to go private — clean dated merger-arb spread to work.

ETFS Capital (10.5%, largest holder) + Lion Point group agreement pushing board seats into the 2026 annual meeting/proxy contest — real activist+structural lane with a dated trigger, but value path uncertain — WATCH.

Live all-stock merger with American Water (announced Oct-2025, both shareholders approved Feb-2026); multiple state regulatory approvals landing through 2026 with close path still open — dated stock-merger-arb with remaining approval gates.

Multiple dated 2026 clinical readouts: WVE-006 AATD 400mg cohort Q1 (gating mid-2026 FDA accelerated-approval engagement) and WVE-007 obesity 240mg single-dose + cohort follow-ups in 1Q/2Q — real dated binaries, asymmetry crowded post-GLP-1 hype so WATCH.
Definitive Ligand acquisition at $39.00/sh cash, expected to close on or about July 14 2026 -- clean dated cash merger-arb.
Live cash merger-arb: Hapag-Lloyd $35.00/sh ($4.2B), 97.36% shareholder approval Apr 2026, pending regulatory + Israel State Share, expected close ~late 2026.
Live merger-arb: definitive Theravance acquisition targeted to close 2H26, plus zanidatamab US PDUFA Aug 25 2026 and $125M buyback — two dated forward catalysts, not fired.

verified.catalyst: Whirlpool's secured-notes refi and 2026/27 note tender are complete and the Appaloosa/Tepper activist campaign has decayed (stake cut from ~7% to ~3%, no proxy contest), leaving no dated forward catalyst.
verified.dated_milestone: Tender offer expired June 30, 2026 (already lapsed); no forward equity catalyst scheduled. Unrelated refrigerator class-action final-approval hearing July 9, 2026 (not an equity event).
verified.bloom_gates: None pass on a forward basis. G1/G2 (the $2B secured notes and tender) are concrete with named structure but already FIRED/priced in June 2026; G3 (Tepper strategic-review demand) is a Feb-2026 letter that has since deflated — activist sold down rather than hardened.
verified.edge: L — the recap is complete and the activist is exiting, not escalating; everything in the triage thesis is backward-looking and already in the tape (stock -66% off year-high to $38).
verified.primary_source: StockTitan/PRNewswire "Whirlpool early tender draws 73% 2026, 91% 2027" + Appaloosa SC 13D (Mar 3, 2026) and Motley Fool/Yahoo on Appaloosa selldown (Mar 19, 2026); price/cap via FMP quote (Jun 29-30, 2026, $38, MC ~$2.46B)
verified.analysis: The deleveraging the triage cites is done, not forward: the $2B 7.5%/7.875% senior secured second-lien notes (2031/2034) priced in early June 2026, the dividend was suspended in May 2026, and the cash tender for the 1.250% 2026 / 1.100% 2027 notes expired June 30, 2026 with 73%/91% already tendered. The "activist hardening" framing is backwards — Appaloosa cut its stake from ~7% to ~3.01% (1.95M shares) by March 31, 2026, reported no longer owning >5%, and its only action was a Feb-25-2026 letter with no proxy contest or board nominees filed. With no specific, dated, currently-unpriced forward catalyst (the only future-dated event is an unrelated refrigerator class-action hearing on July 9, 2026), this fails the strict forward gate.

verified.catalyst: WPP is mid-overhaul under new CEO Cindy Rose (Elevate28: holdco-to-single-company simplification + GBP500m annualised cost-out by 2028) and has hired Goldman Sachs to explore a sale of PR arm Burson (FY25 rev GBP667m, -6% LFL), its first major disposal.
verified.dated_milestone: Next hard date: H1 2026 results 2026-07-31 (potential Burson-process update); Burson review itself has no disclosed completion date — still "advisors hired / exploring options" as of ~2026-04-13.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 partial only — Elevate28 GBP500m cost-out by 2028 is a dated forward commitment but a soft multi-year operational target on a shrinking base, not an arb event. G1 FAIL (no named Burson counterparty), G3 FAIL (no disclosed sale price/value; only FGS-to-KKR GBP1.3bn comp).
verified.edge: M — structural simplification + a real disposal process Goldman is running is genuine and not fully priced (stock 245.7p, near 52wk low 218.5 vs 537 high, ~GBP2.65bn cap), but the read-through is muddied by a melting top line and no hard dated arb.
verified.primary_source: WebSearch + odwyerpr.com "WPP Mulls Burson Divestiture" (2026-04-13) and mmm-online/Campaign "WPP hires advisors for potential Burson sale"; FMP quote WPP.L 245.7p and earnings calendar (next 2026-07-31), accessed 2026-06-30.
verified.analysis: This is a genuine structural-restructuring story — new CEO, holdco-to-single-company simplification, GBP500m cost-out by 2028, and Goldman hired to explore a Burson sale — but it fails strict ACTIVE gating: no named counterparty, no binding deal, no disclosed price, and no committed completion date (still "exploring options" since ~April 2026). The hard upcoming date is H1 results on 2026-07-31, which may update the process but is not itself the catalyst. With a melting top line (FY25/2026 revenue declining) and the upside resting on a not-yet-signed disposal plus a soft multi-year cost target, this is correctly WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash merger: Ares Management to acquire Whitestone REIT at $19.00/share (~$1.7bn), expected Q3-2026 close to take the REIT private.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote pending; deal expected to close Q3-2026 (announced Apr 8/9, 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Ares) + G2 (definitive merger agreement, $19.00/sh cash) satisfied, but G3 fails: no currently-unpriced figure remains — spread is closed.
verified.primary_source: Whitestone/Ares 8-K + press release "Ares Management to Acquire Whitestone REIT for $1.7 Billion," announced Apr 8/9 2026 (stocktitan.net, costar.com, kirkland.com); price via FMP quote 2026-06-30.
verified.analysis: The Ares/Whitestone deal is real, definitive and dated (named counterparty Ares, signed merger agreement at $19.00/sh all-cash, Q3-2026 close), so it passes G1/G2 — but the catalyst already FIRED on the April 8/9 2026 announcement and is fully priced. WSR trades $18.96 against the $19.00 cash price, leaving only $0.04 (~0.21% gross, ~1% annualized over the ~2-3 months to close) — below the risk-free rate and no edge to a non-arb book. This is a picked-clean, re-rated spread with no currently-unpriced figure (G3 fails); not a forward catalyst, so tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: The ETFS Capital / Lion Point activist campaign that the triage flagged has resolved against the activist: the 2026 annual meeting was held June 17, 2026, ETFS filed no 2026 proxy contest, and all nine board-recommended directors won with 97%+ support.
verified.dated_milestone: 2026 Annual Meeting held June 17, 2026 (FIRED, board swept); next stockholder-nomination window for the 2027 meeting opens around the Dec 30, 2026 proposal deadline, but no evidence ETFS intends to re-nominate.
verified.bloom_gates: None fully met. G1 (named counterparty: ETFS Capital/Graham Tuckwell) exists historically but the campaign is dormant; no G2 concrete forward commitment and no G3 specific currently-unpriced figure remain.
verified.edge: L — the triage premise is stale/wrong: stake is ~3.6% (5.25M sh), not 10.5%; Lion Point partnership disbanded; no 2026 proxy contest was run; board re-elected with 97.39-99.95% of votes, so there is no live, dated, forward activist trigger to price.
verified.primary_source: WisdomTree 8-K reporting 2026 annual-meeting voting results (meeting held June 17, 2026), via stocktitan.net/sec-filings/WT and WisdomTree DEF 14A FY2026 (SEC, filed 2026; record date April 23, 2026).
verified.analysis: The triage note describes a 10.5% ETFS Capital stake plus a Lion Point group agreement pushing board seats into a 2026 proxy contest, but the verified record contradicts this: the 2026 annual meeting already occurred on June 17, 2026 (record date April 23), ETFS did not nominate any directors, Lion Point is absent from the 2026 proxy and its partnership with ETFS disbanded, and ETFS/Tuckwell now disclose only ~3.6% (5.25M shares). All nine board-recommended directors were elected with 97.39-99.95% support and the auditor and say-on-pay passed at 99%+, so the activist lane is closed, not forward. The only future structural hook is the routine 2027 nomination window (~Dec 30, 2026 proposal deadline), but there is no dated commitment or evidence ETFS will re-engage, so this fails the dated-forward-unpriced test.

verified.catalyst: Live all-stock merger into American Water (0.305 AWK/WTRG) clearing remaining state PUCs + HSR toward a Q1-2027 close, leaving a thin ~1.2% gross arb spread gated on contested Pennsylvania/New Jersey approvals.
verified.dated_milestone: Remaining-state PUC decisions (notably PA/NJ, contested) landing through 2026; expected deal close end of Q1 2027.
verified.instrument: equity (WTRG common; arb vs short AWK at 0.305x)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: American Water/AWK) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed all-stock merger, 0.305x ratio, both shareholder votes passed Feb-2026)
verified.edge: L — gross spread only ~1.2% (implied ~$37.68 vs WTRG ~$37.23) over ~9mo (~1.6% annualized); the spread already prices the remaining-approval risk, and contested PA/NJ PUCs (OCA/PULP protests) are the live break-risk, so there is little un-priced figure (weak G3).
verified.primary_source: PR Newswire / American Water IR — "Merger Progresses with Approval from the Virginia State Corporation Commission" (Jun 22, 2026); PA PUC public-input hearing notices (Mar 19 / Apr 7, 2026)
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, dated and forward: a signed all-stock merger into American Water (0.305 AWK per WTRG), shareholder-approved Feb-2026, with Kentucky (Apr 21), Ohio (May 13) and Virginia (Jun 22, 2026) approvals already secured and remaining state PUCs plus HSR still open into a Q1-2027 close. But it is a plain merger-arb with only a ~1.2% gross spread over ~9 months, and that spread exists precisely because the contested Pennsylvania/New Jersey commissions (formal OCA and PULP protests, public-input hearings underway) carry genuine break-risk. Genuine dated event, but low edge and asymmetric to the downside if a major state blocks, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: WVE-006 (AATD RNA-editing) FDA accelerated-approval pathway engagement, with company-guided regulatory feedback expected mid-2026, is the remaining forward binary; most other 2026 readouts have already fired.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA accelerated-approval engagement feedback for WVE-006 — guided "mid-2026" (next discrete event; no hard date disclosed). WVE-007 400mg 6-mo / 600mg 3-mo obesity follow-ups guided 2Q 2026 (now at quarter boundary, likely imminent/just-reported).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: company committed to FDA accelerated-approval engagement with mid-2026 feedback after regaining full WVE-006 rights on 2026-02-02). Partial G1 (named counterparty = FDA) but no hard-dated meeting/PDUFA; G3 fails — no specific currently-unpriced figure, stock already at 52-wk low pricing in skepticism.
verified.primary_source: Wave Life Sciences press releases: "Plans to Accelerate Regulatory Engagement with Full Control of WVE-006" (GlobeNewswire, 2026-02-02; mid-2026 FDA feedback guidance) and Q1 2026 financial results (2026-04-28); Phase 2a initiation PR (2026-06-24). FMP quote $5.68 (2026-06-30).
verified.analysis: As of 2026-06-30, most of the triage-cited dated binaries have already FIRED: WVE-007 240mg 6-month obesity data (Mar 26, 2026), WVE-006 RestorAATion-2 400mg monthly MZ-like data (May 18, 2026), and Phase 2a INLIGHT initiation (Jun 24, 2026); WVE has de-rated to $5.68 near its $5.02 52-week low (vs $21.73 high), so prior readouts already disappointed and asymmetry is no longer clean. The remaining genuine forward catalyst is the WVE-006 FDA accelerated-approval engagement with guided mid-2026 feedback (a concrete G2 commitment following the Feb 2, 2026 rights-regain), plus residual 2Q-guided WVE-007 400mg/600mg follow-ups now at the quarter boundary. Because the principal milestone is soft-dated regulatory feedback rather than a hard dated/unpriced binary, this clears WATCH but not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Ligand's definitive all-cash acquisition of XOMA Royalty at $39.00/sh plus one non-transferable CVR (75% of net proceeds from XOMA's TREMFYA litigation vs J&J), vote July 13 2026, expected close Q3 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Special Meeting stockholder vote July 13, 2026; expected close Q3 2026 (not "July 14" close)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Ligand) + G2 (signed definitive merger agreement at $39.00/sh) are met, but the cash leg is already through-priced so the catalyst is effectively fired/re-rated for the arb thesis; only the CVR remains and it fails G3 (no specific currently-unpriced dated figure — litigation proceeds are contingent and undated).
verified.edge: L — the "clean dated cash merger-arb at $39.00" thesis is contradicted by the tape: XOMA trades $42.15, i.e. $3.15 ABOVE the cash price, so the cash arb is negative (guaranteed loss to close on cash alone). Residual upside is purely the speculative, undated CVR litigation option, which the market is already pricing.
verified.primary_source: XOMA DEFM14A merger proxy + Ligand/XOMA 8-K (stocktitan, SEC EDGAR), deal announced Apr 28 2026; FMP quote $42.15 vs $39.00 cash, as of 2026-06-30
verified.analysis: The merger is genuine, signed, and dated (Ligand/XOMA at $39.00/sh cash + CVR; vote July 13 2026, close Q3 2026), but the triage's "clean dated cash merger-arb" framing is broken because XOMA trades at $42.15 — well above the $39.00 cash consideration, so there is no positive cash arb spread and a buyer locking the cash leg loses ~$3/sh. The entire premium reflects the contingent, undated CVR tied to XOMA's TREMFYA litigation against J&J, which is speculative optionality rather than a clean dated forward cash catalyst, and is already priced in. Net: the dated cash event is real but effectively re-rated/through-priced and not investable as an arb; the only remaining catalyst (litigation CVR) is undated and contingent, so it fails strict gating.

verified.catalyst: Live cash merger-arb: Hapag-Lloyd to acquire ZIM at $35.00/share (~$4.2B), 97.36% shareholder-approved Apr 30 2026, pending Israeli Golden Share consent + antitrust clearances, expected close ~late 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Israeli State Special (Golden) Share consent + regulatory clearances (DOJ/FTC, EU DG COMP, Singapore CCCS); next gate = Israeli govt position decision; expected deal close ~late 2026 / Q4 2026.
verified.instrument: ZIM common (NYSE), long vs. $35.00 cash deal price
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Hapag-Lloyd AG), G2 (concrete binding commitment: signed merger agreement at $35.00/sh, 97.36% shareholder-approved), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ~$35 vs ~$24.75 px = ~$10/sh, ~37% gross spread)
verified.edge: M — quantified ~37% gross spread to a 97%-approved binding deal is large and specific (clear unpriced gap), but the spread is fat precisely because the gating event is a binary Israeli political Golden Share decision (govt has not taken a position) with a live competing $4.5B/$37.50 Sakal bid; not a routine antitrust clearance, so completion risk is real and political, not modelable.
verified.primary_source: ZIM/PRNewswire deal announcement ($35.00/sh, $4.2B, Feb 17 2026) + Seatrade-Maritime "Zim says Hapag-Lloyd agreement binding after surprise rival bid" (May 2026) + Splash247/Maritime Executive on Sakal $4.5B/$37.50 bid + Israeli govt yet to take Golden Share position (as of late June 2026)
verified.kill_fact: Hapag-Lloyd/ZIM $35.00/share cash deal (~$4.2B) is real, signed, and binding: 97.36% shareholder-approved Apr 30 2026 (JoC/Calcalist), ZIM board re-confirmed binding after the rival bid. Still forward, not fired/broken: ZIM trades ~$22-26 in June 2026 vs $35 cash (~35-50% gross spread). The wide spread is genuinely unpriced political/regulatory risk, not a stale line: (1) State of Israel 'Golden Share' consent explicitly NOT yet granted; (2) competing Haim Sakal $37.50/share ($4.5B) bid (May 6) rejected by board as the Hapag agreement is binding (break-fee window closed post-vote). All three Bloom gates clear: named acquirer (Hapag-Lloyd), concrete signed/shareholder-approved agreement, specific quantified live spread. Minor correction: deal was signed/announced Feb 16 2026, not 'late 2026' — late 2026 is the expected CLOSE.
verified.analysis: The Hapag-Lloyd/ZIM $35.00/share cash deal is real, binding, and 97.36% shareholder-approved (Apr 30 2026), trading ~$24.75 (Jun 1) for a ~37% gross spread that is genuinely unpriced and forward (deal not closed/broken as of late June 2026). All three Bloom gates clear: named acquirer, concrete signed agreement, specific quantified spread. The unusually wide spread reflects two live risks: (1) the State of Israel's Special "Golden Share" consent, which the government has explicitly not yet granted, and (2) a competing $4.5B/$37.50 all-cash Sakal Group bid (May 6) that ZIM's board rejected as non-binding but which pressures the Israeli political sign-off — making this an event-driven arb gated on a political decision rather than a clean rate-and-time spread.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision Aug 25, 2026 on expanding partner Jazz's zanidatamab (Ziihera) into first-line HER2+ gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma, which on approval releases a contractual $250M milestone to Zymeworks.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA PDUFA target action date 2026-08-25 for the first-line HER2+ GEA sBLA (Priority Review accepted, Breakthrough Therapy Designation in hand, HERIZON-GEA-01 Phase 3 positive); next date 2026-08-25.
verified.instrument: equity (defined-risk event position; binary regulatory bet, size accordingly)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA regulator + partner Jazz Pharmaceuticals), G2 (concrete commitment: fixed contractual $250.0M Jazz milestone on US approval, plus $15M China milestone), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: $250M cash ~14% of ~$1.8B cap, treated by market as a 50/50 coin-flip vs ~70/30 de-risked).
verified.edge: M — clean dated regulator-set date ~57 days out with a fixed contractual payout and a cash-backed floor (~$404M cash + non-recourse $250M Royalty Pharma note, funded beyond 2028), but it is a genuinely binary CRL/extension risk and the name has already run, capping asymmetry to roughly 2-to-1.
verified.primary_source: Pearce IP, "FDA Grants Priority Review to sBLA for Jazz/BeOne's zanidatamab... First-Line HER2+ GEA," 2026-04-27 (PDUFA 2026-08-25); Zymeworks Q1-2026 8-K/press release 2026-05 ($250M Jazz milestone, $403.8M cash, $125M buyback); BioSpace "Zymeworks to Acquire Theravance Biopharma," 2026-06-29.
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm the load-bearing catalyst: Jazz PR (Apr 27, 2026) sets the FDA PDUFA target action date of Aug 25, 2026 for Ziihera (zanidatamab-hrii) sBLA in first-line HER2+ locally advanced/metastatic GEA, accepted under Priority Review with Breakthrough Therapy Designation, supported by positive Phase 3 HERIZON-GEA-01. Zymeworks Q1 2026 confirms it is entitled to a $250M milestone from Jazz upon US regulatory approval. Forward and not fired (decision ~8 weeks out as of 2026-06-30). CORRECTION: the buyback magnitude is misstated — Zymeworks Q1 2026 says ~$96M of the $125M program already utilized (not $35.4M executed); peripheral, non-load-bearing. Theravance/TBPH arb framing correctly flagged as misapplied (ZYME is the all-cash acquirer, not the target), but catalyst does not depend on it.
verified.analysis: The genuine catalyst is the FDA PDUFA on Aug 25, 2026 for first-line HER2+ GEA, confirmed by Jazz/Pearce IP and Zymeworks' Q1-2026 filing: Priority Review, Breakthrough Therapy Designation, positive HERIZON-GEA-01 Phase 3, and a fixed $250M Jazz milestone on approval — real, dated, forward, not fired. The triage flag's "merger_arb" framing is misapplied: ZYME is the ACQUIRER of Theravance ($17/sh, $929M all-cash, close 2H26, subject to TBPH shareholder vote) — outbound cash deployment that erodes the floor, not a takeout of ZYME, so any arb spread lives in TBPH shares, not ZYME; the $125M buyback (authorized May 14, $35.4M executed) is an ongoing/fired capital-return, not a forward dated event. Catalyst stands on the regulatory date alone; lane corrected to fda_biotech.
